title: Hypobathrum racemosum
taxonomy: Hypobathrum racemosum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hypobathrum, which is a group of plants commonly known as the 'pimpinella'. The classification of Hypobathrum racemosum places it as a member of the order Gentianales. This order consists of flowering plants that are often found in tropical regions and are commonly known as the 'bottlebrush family'. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes species commonly known as coffee, gardenias, and jasmine.

intro: Hypobathrum racemosum, also known as Java tea, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and diarrhea, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hypobathrum racemosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Hypobathrum racemosum is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the preparation of herbal capsules and tablets.
